name: Shootash
overview: Shootash is a small hamlet in the north of Wellow civil parish in the Test Valley district of Hampshire, strung along the A27 Romsey-to-Whiteparish road about 2.5 miles west of Romsey. Originally just a few farmsteads, woodland and common land, it developed into a small roadside hamlet through the 20th century. The village of West Wellow lies about 2 miles south-west, and the New Forest is close by. Shootash has no shops or pub of its own, with everyday and fuller amenities in Romsey, including two supermarkets, schools and a railway station.
character: A small, dispersed roadside hamlet of houses, bungalows, barn conversions and farms in open Test Valley countryside on the edge of the New Forest. The feel is quiet and rural, though the A27 runs through it; many properties sit on generous plots along lanes off the main road.
amenities: No shop or pub within the hamlet
Two supermarkets, shops, schools and leisure facilities in Romsey (~2.5 mi)
Romsey railway station (~2.5 mi) for South Western and cross-country services
New Forest National Park nearby for walking and riding
Pubs in nearby Sherfield English and West Wellow

commutes.notes: Nearest town, via the A27; two supermarkets and a station
From Romsey (~2.5 mi) via Southampton/Basingstoke connections

prices.summary: Shootash housing is mostly detached houses, bungalows and barn conversions on good-sized plots; the SO51 market around Romsey generally runs from the mid-£400ks for smaller homes up to £1m+ for larger country properties, with limited turnover in the hamlet itself.

thingsToDo: Walking and riding in the nearby New Forest National Park
Test Valley footpaths and country lanes
Romsey town, Broadlands estate and Romsey Abbey
placesToEat: Pubs in Sherfield English and West Wellow (within ~2 mi)
Wide choice of pubs, cafes and restaurants in Romsey (~2.5 mi)
New Forest pubs and inns a short drive south
pros: Quiet rural hamlet close to Romsey and the New Forest
Romsey's full amenities and station within ~2.5 miles
Good road access to Southampton and Salisbury
cons: A27 through-traffic and road noise for some properties
No shops, pub or school within the hamlet itself
No fast direct London rail link without connections
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a rural, plot-rich location on the edge of the New Forest with quick access to Romsey's amenities and station, who don't mind being on a main road.
